heading: Use of funds for ITS activities
label: 513 Use of funds for ITS activities
notes: Amendments2012— amended section generally. Prior to amendment, section read as follows:Pub. L. 112–141“(a) .—For each fiscal year, not more than $250,000 of the funds made available to carry out this subtitle C of title V of the SAFETEA–LU shall be used for intelligent transportation system outreach, public relations, displays, tours, and brochures.In General“(b) .—Subsection (a) shall not apply to intelligent transportation system training, scholarships, or the publication or distribution of research findings, technical guidance, or similar documents.”Applicability

Effective Date of 2012 AmendmentAmendment by effective , see , set out as an Effective and Termination Dates of 2012 Amendment note under .Pub. L. 112–141section 3(a) of Pub. L. 112–141section 101 of this titleOct. 1, 2012

children.heading: .—Eligible entity
children.label: 1 .—Eligible entity
children.text: The term “eligible entity” means a State or local government, tribal government, transit agency, public toll authority, metropolitan planning organization, other political subdivision of a State or local government, or a multistate or multijurisdictional group applying through a single lead applicant.

children.label: A
children.text: have signed a written agreement to implement an activity that meets the grant criteria under this section; and

children.label: B
children.text: is comprised of at least 2 members, each of whom is an eligible entity.

children.heading: .—Purpose
children.label: b .—Purpose
children.text: The purpose of this section is to develop, administer, communicate, and promote the use of products of research, technology, and technology transfer programs.

children.label: D
children.text: other tools, strategies, or methods that will result in the deployment of innovative ITS technologies.

children.heading: .—Comprehensive plan
children.label: 2 .—Comprehensive plan
children.text: To carry out this section, the Secretary shall develop a detailed and comprehensive plan that addresses the manner in which incentives may be adopted, as appropriate, through the existing deployment activities carried out by surface transportation modal administrations.
